reviews.review_msg: His lectures were interesting but lacked diverse perspectives, leading to controversial points being made. Unfortunately he wasn't as open to criticism and feedback as he had previously stated. The TA was amazing, she wasn't afraid to bring up controversial points made in lecture and gave great feedback when asked.

As a 1st year, I took his class for Education. I enjoyed listening to Bingham's lectures, although it seemed like they weren't fully planned, but he always knew what to talk about. The TA I got was a tough grader, but she is really nice and understandable. I learned more from her tutorial class than Bingham's lectures. Final project had no rubric.
This class was very interesting. There are weekly logs based on the lectures and readings. He is an easy grader, but make sure to do good on the final project, as it is worth 40% of the final grade.
chill and caring prof, some of the logs were marked sort of harshly, but overall great course grading scale is cooked though

The best I professor ive ever had! He's an easy marker and super funny. Most of the time it is easy to get by without doing any of the readings assigned as he basically summarizes everything important the next class. He makes learning fun and never puts too much pressure on a student. He also watched movies with us and tells stories, Love him!
The funniest, most caring professor ever. He makes learning so much fun and his classes are so engaging. Three hours fly by with him as the professor.

Both the Prof and the TA are so nice. They give really good feedback and are always willing to help. The course itself isn't hard at all, very manageable. Honestly one of the most chill classes I've taken

I've never written a review but he deserves it. I'm not finished the course yet but Bing and the TA's genuinely want their students to succeed. All you have to do is go to class (he does do attendance) and go to tuts. Tuts are super easy the first half is literally basically just grounding exercises. Take this class if you want to boost your gpa!

Bing really is a super caring and nice teacher. His lectures are always engaging and he makes me want to learn more. I felt happy coming to his class. Super kind person and I would recommend him highly!

He is amazing. Yes he goes on tangents but he cares about everything he teaches. Easily the best prof I've had at SFU highly recommend. Tutorials will go over the readings and the lectures are their own thing.

His lectures are highly unprepared and he just keeps yapping about his personal life. Interesting readings but we don't really talk about them. The tutorial addresses the readings more than the lecture. No slides. Pretends everything is a big revelation when it's the most basic thing we have all heard about already.

Genuinely speaking, Bingham is a one of a kind academic inspiration unlike any other that I have ever witnessed in undergrad. Lecture concepts share a common philosophical thread that all tie into the course themes in which he explains and reiterates thoroughly throughout the semester. Workload was quite easy and the marking was reasonable.

Lectures are really engaging and the course concepts are really interesting. Weekly readings and logs and a project for grading, but very do-able and you have a lot of freedom to write about what you want as long as you refer to readings and lecture. Funny guy but rambles a lot. Lecture is mandatory as you have to input attendance to get a grade.
Such an unorganized prof with the most unorganized TAS. The lectures were super random and all over the place. Never had slides. He just rambled forever. Worst class I have ever taken by far.

Charles is the most wholesome professor. He is so kind hearted and truly cares about his students. He is the best professor I have had at SFU and he makes me feel like this is how school and teachers should all be like. Caring of your mental health, understanding, and patient. He understands that we are just human and we make mistakes.
